fr.recommendations: Enhancing the clarity and width of pedestrian walkways may facilitate smoother navigation for emergency responders on the property. Ensuring these pathways are clearly marked and unobstructed could support rapid access to the main building in case of an emergency. Additionally, maintaining walkways free of debris or obstructions may enhance the efficiency of responder movement, allowing them to carry equipment more easily and respond effectively to the situations that may arise. Improvements in these areas could also ensure safe and efficient operational staging close to the main building, further boosting the effectiveness of any emergency interventions.

wildfire.conditions: Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetated Perimeter
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property by increasing separation between vegetation and structures could improve wildfire resilience. Actively managing and reducing the density of vegetation directly adjacent to the building may reduce fuel continuity and lower the potential exposure of the structure to wildfire. It would be beneficial to focus on trimming or removing vegetation along access routes to support firefighter access and ease suppression efforts. Moreover, a concentrated effort on managing the vegetated perimeter of the property by reducing fuel loads and maintaining open spaces could aid in creating effective fuel breaks. These actions collectively may enhance the long-term preparedness of the property and help maintain safer conditions during a wildfire event.
